title: Pizza Problem: Adding Fractions with Like Denominators

steps.instruction: Jake ate 2/6 of a pizza and his brother ate 3/6 of the same pizza. How much pizza was eaten in total?
steps.explanation: The word 'total' tells us to add the two amounts together.

steps.instruction: Both fractions have denominator 6. Add the numerators.
Write the answer as a sentence.
steps.work: They ate 5/6 of the pizza in total.
steps.explanation: Always relate the fraction back to the original problem. 5/6 of the pizza was eaten.

steps.instruction: A jar is 7/10 full of marbles. After adding more, it is 9/10 full. What fraction of the jar was added?
steps.explanation: We know the start and end amounts. The added amount is the difference.

steps.subgoalLabel: Subtract to find the missing part
steps.explanation: Subtracting the starting amount from the ending amount gives us what was added.
2/10 simplifies to 1/5 (divide both by 2). One-fifth of the jar was filled.
